frame_analyses.description: Initial view showing FLS trainer with peg transfer board visible, no cutting task setup visible yet
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be showing wrong task setup initially

frame_analyses.description: Instruments entering field, graspers approaching the white board/gauze
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Two graspers visible entering from ports

frame_analyses.description: Right grasper appears to be grasping gauze while left instrument begins cutting motion at center position
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial cut starting at approximately 12 o'clock position on circle

frame_analyses.description: Cutting progressing clockwise, appears to be following the marked circular pattern
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Steady progression around circle, maintaining tension with non-cutting hand

frame_analyses.description: Continued clockwise cutting, approximately at 3 o'clock position
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good coordination between instruments, cut appears to follow line reasonably well

frame_analyses.description: Cutting continuing around circle, approaching 6 o'clock position
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent cutting motion, gauze remains secured

frame_analyses.description: Cutting at approximately 9 o'clock position, nearing completion of circle
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Maintaining steady progression, no obvious deviations from line

frame_analyses.description: Final view showing LaproTrain system, task appears completed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: External view of trainer suggesting task completion

confidence_rationale: Video quality and camera angles make precise measurement of cutting deviation impossible. Cannot determine exact mm deviations from marked line at cardinal points. Completion time estimate based on visible instrument activity, but exact timing uncertain due to video quality.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation measurements at 8 cardinal points - camera resolution and angle insufficient
Precise cutting line adherence - marked circle not clearly visible in frames
Exact completion time - video transitions make precise timing difficult
technique_summary: Intermediate performance with systematic clockwise cutting approach. Good instrument coordination and gauze tension maintenance. Completion time of 85 seconds falls within intermediate range (90-180s benchmark).
strengths: Good coordination between cutting and stabilizing instruments
improvement_suggestions: Camera quality prevents assessment of cutting precision - this would be the primary area for improvement feedback
Consider slower, more deliberate cutting to ensure precision within 2mm tolerance
